mechanism_narrative: Elevated federal funds rate above 5% constrains corporate borrowing costs and reduces equity valuations through higher discount rates. This persistent monetary tightness produces negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days as market reprices growth expectations downward.
evidence_refs: fact:current FEDFUNDS at 5.26% indicates active tightening regime
regime_hint: monetary tightening cycle with rates above neutral
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day returns in > 65% of instances when FEDFUNDS > 5.0%, or Rank IC < 0.02 over any 2-year rolling window during tightening periods.

mechanism_narrative: Rising unemployment rate signals deteriorating labor market conditions and reduced consumer spending capacity. A 0.3 percentage point increase in UNRATE over 3 months predicts negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days as earnings expectations decline.
evidence_refs: fact:current UNRATE at 4.5% provides baseline for deterioration detection
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 60-day returns in > 60% of triggered instances despite unemployment rising > 0.3pp, or correlation between UNRATE changes and forward returns becomes statistically insignificant (p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: Retail sales growth (RSAFS) directly reflects consumer demand strength and corporate revenue expectations. Sustained month-over-month growth in RSAFS above 0.5% signals robust demand and produces positive S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days as earnings revisions flow upward.
failure_condition: Rank IC between RSAFS momentum and 5-day forward returns falls below 0.04 over any 1-year rolling window, or positive RSAFS growth fails to predict positive 5-day returns in > 55% of instances.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production growth reflects real economic capacity utilization and manufacturing health. When INDPRO declines month-over-month, it signals supply-side weakness and stagflationary pressure, producing negative S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days as profit margins compress.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day returns in > 62% of instances when INDPRO declines > 0.2% month-over-month, or Spearman rank correlation < 0.05 over any 2-year window.

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts (HOUST) and retail sales (RSAFS) jointly capture consumer confidence and discretionary spending capacity. When both HOUST and RSAFS show synchronized month-over-month growth, the multiplier effect on corporate earnings is amplified, producing positive S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days. The mechanism fails if either series declines.
evidence_refs: fact:current HOUST at 1408 and RSAFS at 370495 both near trigger thresholds
regime_hint: expansion phase with synchronized demand growth
failure_condition: Joint HOUST-RSAFS growth fails to predict positive 20-day returns in > 58% of triggered instances, or interaction term coefficient becomes statistically insignificant (p > 0.15) in rolling 2-year regressions.

mechanism_narrative: Payroll employment (PAYEMS) growth combined with stable inflation (CPIAUCSL) signals a Goldilocks scenario where the Fed can maintain accommodative policy. When PAYEMS grows > 150k month-over-month AND CPIAUCSL year-over-year growth remains below 3%, S&P 500 forward returns are positive over 60 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:current PAYEMS at 137410 thousand persons, CPIAUCSL at 203.153
regime_hint: soft-landing scenario with balanced growth and inflation control
failure_condition: Goldilocks regime fails to produce positive 60-day returns in > 60% of instances, or interaction coefficient becomes insignificant (p > 0.12) when tested over rolling 3-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: When federal funds rate (FEDFUNDS) exceeds 5% AND inflation (CPIAUCSL) shows no month-over-month decline, the Fed is in restrictive hold mode. This regime produces negative S&P 500 returns over 5 trading days as markets price in extended rate persistence and delayed rate cuts.
evidence_refs: fact:FEDFUNDS at 5.26% and CPIAUCSL at 203.153 suggest restrictive hold
regime_hint: monetary tightening persistence with sticky inflation
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 5-day returns in > 58% of triggered instances, or the interaction term between FEDFUNDS and CPIAUCSL momentum becomes statistically insignificant (p > 0.15).

mechanism_narrative: Real GDP growth (GDPC1) is the ultimate demand signal. In expansion regimes (GDPC1 growing > 2% annualized), S&P 500 forward returns are systematically positive over 60 trading days. In contraction regimes (GDPC1 growth < 0%), returns are negative. This regime-conditional mechanism flips direction based on growth regime.
evidence_refs: fact:current GDPC1 at 11506.5 billion chained 2017 dollars
regime_hint: expansion phase with positive real GDP growth above 2% annualized
failure_condition: SPX 60-day returns in expansion regimes (GDPC1 growth > 2%) are not significantly higher than contraction regimes (t-test p > 0.10), or Rank IC < 0.05 over any 3-year rolling window.
